title: Unlimited private chat creation enables resource exhaustion
description: The createPrivateChat method (line 197) creates private chats without any limit on the number of chats a single user can create. A malicious user with wired.account.chat.create_chats privilege can create millions of PrivateChat objects. Each object is stored in-memory in self.chats, self.privateChats, and consumes a UInt32 chatID (lastChatID increments). Additionally, since lastChatID is a UInt32, creating 2^32 chats would overflow to 0 and potentially collide with existing chat IDs including the protected public chat (chatID=1).
recommendation: Add a per-user limit on active private chats (e.g., 50). Add a global limit on total chats. Guard against UInt32 overflow in nextChatID() by checking for wrap-around and refusing to create new chats when the ID space is exhausted.
